basics.name: Hung Phan Quoc Viet
basics.label: Undergraduate Student
basics.summary: A junior studying Computer Science and Applied Mathematics at University of Utah, interested in computer graphics and data visualization.

work.name: School of Dentistry, University of Utah
highlights: Designed a MySQL database for 1TB of health data, optimizing for efficient storage and query performance.
Prepared manuscripts and implemented statistical analysis methods in Python using Jupyter Lab.
Employed mathematical modeling, software development, big data analysis, and high-performance computing to study pathogen emergence, spread, and existing epidemics.

work.name: Kahlert School of Computing, University of Utah
highlights: Guided students through data structures and algorithms concepts and assignments during office hours.
Provided grades and detailed feedback for assignments, emphasizing code efficiency and best practices.

work.name: Scientific Computing and Imaging Institute
highlights: Developed a Visual Studio Code extension tool for seamless integration and manipulation of graph data structures.
Implemented a robust feature set for exporting graph data structures to DOT language formats.
Enhanced teaching effectiveness by integrating interactive visualizations, facilitating a deeper understanding of graph data structures for students.

work.name: LSTS Robotics Team
highlights: Won second place in World Robot Olympiad Competition National level by working with two team members to develop a solution using LEGO Mindstorms to solve a specific problem given by the Association.

Achieved top 4 ranking nationally in First Lego League Competition by leading a six-member team to research and address a real-world issue, effectively presenting findings using LEGO Mindstorms technology.
Implemented and fine-tuned the PID algorithm, a widely recognized and extensively used control algorithm in WRO and FLL, to optimize robot accuracy and performance.

volunteer.summary: Lead organizer for the New York City branch of the People's Climate March, the largest climate march in history.
highlights: Awarded 'Climate Hero' award by Greenpeace for my efforts organizing the march.
Men of the year 2014 by Time magazine

awards.summary: Awarded to one teaching assistant in the School of Computing per school year in recognition of exceptional teaching skills and genuine concern for students.

publications.name: Zur Elektrody/namik bewegter Körper
publications.summary: It concerned an interpretation of the Michelson–Morley experiment and the properties of light and time. Special relativity incorporates the principle that the speed of light is the same for all inertial observers regardless of the state of motion of the source.

publications.name: Über einen die Erzeugung und Verwandlung des Lichtes betreffenden heuristischen Gesichtspunkt
publications.summary: In the second paper, he applied the quantum theory to light to explain the photoelectric effect. In particular, he used the idea of light quanta (photons) to explain experimental results, but stressed the importance of the experimental results. The importance of his work on the photoelectric effect earned him the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921.

publications.name: Die Grundlage der allgemeinen Relativitätstheorie
publications.summary: The publication of the theory of general relativity made him internationally famous. He was professor of physics at the universities of Zurich (1909–1911) and Prague (1911–1912), before he returned to ETH Zurich (1912–1914).

projects.name: Book Keeping Web Application
highlights: Developed a bookkeeping web application enabling users to import Goodreads data and store it on Firebase, featuring login/signup functionality
Implemented data visualization using D3.js to display users’ Goodreads data in an engaging and informative manner.
Integrated APIs for efficient retrieval of book recommendations, popular books of the week, and currently reading lists.

projects.name: Learning Management System
highlights: Designed and implemented a learning management system web application with key features such as user authentication, assignment submission, class enrollment, grading, and class management.
Implemented user authentication and role-based access for students, professors, and admins.
Ensured a stable deployment on a Linux platform, enhancing the overall system performance and reliability.

projects.name: Sprite Editor Software
highlights: Collaborated in a team of 6 using the Waterfall development method to create a Sprite Editor Software.
Implemented core features in C++ and Qt Creator, including color change, brush size adjustment, undo/redo, eraser, and save/load/export functions.
Enhanced user experience with essential sprite editing tools for efficient and intuitive sprite creation.

projects.name: VS Code Graph Visualization Extension
highlights: Created a VS Code extension for visualizing graph structures with an intuitive interface for node and edge creation.
Implemented features such as undo/redo actions, selective erasing, and full canvas deletion.
Deployed the project through Azure to the VS Code extension marketplace, enabling widespread availability.

projects.name: Snake Multiplayer Game
highlights: Teamed up with a colleague to develop a real-time multiplayer snake game, adhering to the MVC architecture.
Developed a robust networking infrastructure for the multiplayer functionality, utilizing TCP/IP sockets.
Optimized the game to accomodate a maximum of 20 concurrent players without latency issue.

projects.name: 3D CPU Rendering Software
highlights: Developed a 3D renderer, with the capability to process Blender-exported meshes through JSON.
Executed precise 3D mesh filling using rasterization and Z-buffer, ensuring depth precision.
Implemented Flat Shading for precise control over lighting, enhancing realism and visual appeal.

projects.name: Data Wrangling Portfolio Project
highlights: Scraped and cleaned 40MB of data from a movie database, for data analysis purposes.
Analyzed the correlation and causation effect of each variable (e.g. movie release date, genre, popularity) to the score.
Developed a predictive model with an 80% accuracy score for forecasting movie scores.
